systemOverview.description: A three-tier architecture consisting of pure MCP skills for stock analysis, a PostgreSQL database for storing analysis results, and a React/Next.js web dashboard for visualization. MCP clients provide their own data, receive analysis results, which are automatically stored and made available through the dashboard.
keyComponents: MCP Skills Layer - Pure analysis skills following Agent Skills spec
Data Processing Layer - Validates and processes client data
Storage Layer - PostgreSQL database for analysis history
API Layer - REST API for data access
Real-time Layer - WebSocket for live updates

architecturalPrinciples: Skills are pure and stateless - no side effects
Client data sovereignty - clients control their data
Automatic persistence - all analyses are stored
Multi-tenant isolation - client data is segregated
Performance at scale - handle millions of records
Financial accuracy - minimum 6 decimal places precision
Audit trail - complete traceability of all calculations
Fail-safe defaults - conservative assumptions when uncertain
Transparency - all calculations must be explainable
Regulatory compliance - meet financial industry standards
Risk-aware - explicit handling of uncertainty and confidence

componentDesign.name: PureSkillsProvider
componentDesign.description: Collection of 14 pure analysis skills exposed via MCP
interfaces: MCP tool interface for each skill

componentDesign.name: AnalysisStorageService
componentDesign.description: Service that intercepts and stores all analysis requests and results

componentDesign.name: PostgreSQLDatabase
componentDesign.description: Relational database storing all analysis data

componentDesign.name: RESTAPIServer
componentDesign.description: Express/FastAPI server providing data access endpoints

componentDesign.name: DashboardUI
componentDesign.description: React/Next.js web application for data visualization

componentDesign.name: WebSocketManager
componentDesign.description: Manages real-time connections for live updates

componentDesign.name: FinancialCalculationEngine
componentDesign.description: High-precision financial calculation engine with audit trail
responsibilities: Handle edge cases and special scenarios

componentDesign.name: RiskAssessmentModule
componentDesign.description: Evaluates and quantifies risks in analysis

componentDesign.name: ComplianceEngine
componentDesign.description: Ensures regulatory compliance and generates required disclosures

componentDesign.name: DataQualityValidator
componentDesign.description: Validates and scores data quality before analysis

dataFlow: MCP client prepares stock data (OHLCV, fundamentals, etc.)
Client calls MCP skill with data
Skill processes data and returns analysis
Storage service intercepts and saves to database
WebSocket broadcasts new analysis to dashboard
Users query and export data via API

dataModels.name: Analysis
dataModels.fields: id, client_id, skill_name, ticker, input_data, result, timestamp, metadata

dataArchitecture.storageStrategy: PostgreSQL with JSONB for flexible schema, indexed for performance

securityArchitecture.authenticationMethod: API keys for MCP, JWT for dashboard
dataProtection: Client data isolation via row-level security

deploymentArchitecture.deploymentModel: Container-based with Docker Compose or Kubernetes
deploymentArchitecture.scalingStrategy: Horizontal scaling for stateless components, vertical for database

architecturalDecisions.title: Use PostgreSQL for analysis storage
architecturalDecisions.context: Need reliable, scalable storage for analysis history
architecturalDecisions.decision: Use PostgreSQL with JSONB for flexible schema
architecturalDecisions.rationale: Mature, reliable, supports complex queries and JSONB for flexibility

architecturalDecisions.title: React/Next.js for dashboard
architecturalDecisions.decision: Build dashboard with React and Next.js
architecturalDecisions.rationale: Modern stack, good performance, rich ecosystem, SSR support

architecturalDecisions.title: Intercept pattern for storage
architecturalDecisions.context: Need to store analyses without modifying skills
architecturalDecisions.decision: Intercept MCP responses to store results
architecturalDecisions.rationale: Keeps skills pure while enabling automatic storage

architecturalDecisions.title: Multi-tenant via client_id
architecturalDecisions.decision: Use client_id field for row-level security
architecturalDecisions.rationale: Simple, effective isolation without separate databases
